prompt:
A small differential-drive robot is shown on a workbench with one of its wheels removed or partially detached, revealing an encoder disk and an optical or magnetic sensor being installed on the axle. A hand with a screwdriver or hex key is visible, carefully securing the encoder hardware in place. Cables from the encoder run neatly toward a microcontroller board or wiring hub on the robot chassis, emphasizing that the wheel sensors are being added for more precise motion tracking.

The scene is a bright, organized robotics studio with a solarpunk atmosphere: warm, diffuse lighting, green plants on nearby shelves, and clearly labeled but unreadable storage bins in the background. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no visible logos or readable text, and a composition that focuses on the wheel, encoder assembly, and careful installation, while the rest of the workspace is softly blurred to suggest a safe, methodical build process.
